rulings.Case Title: VINCENT CASTILLO MARENTES ET AL VS. CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF VINCENT MARENTES, LIUDMILA BICHEGKUEVA's Motion For Reconsideration. Plaintiffs motion for reconsideration is off calendar. The court entered judgment in favor of defendant Crusader Insurance Company on August 7, 2019. The court therefore no longer has the power to rule on the reconsideration motion. (See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236 ["[A]fter entry of judgment, a trial court has no further power to rule on a motion for reconsideration."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) ET AL VS. DIMITRIOU &ASSOCIATES, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Batroa Profit Sharing Plan, Michael Levine'S Motion To Compel Written Responses To Rpds And Production Of Documents, Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions, And Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories From Dimitriou And Associates, P.C. And For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 5, Plaintiffs Batroa Profit Sharing Plan, Michael Levine's Motion To Compel Written Responses To RPDs And Production Of Documents, Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions, And Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories From Dimitriou And Associates, P.C. And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. On or before 4pm Plaintiff's counsel is to email a complete set of the endorsed filed moving papers - which do not appear to be accessible on the Court's website - and the proposed order to the Judge pro tem at rmead@folgerlevin.com. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA TORRES VS. EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPEMENT DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPEMENT DEPARTMENT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Employment Development Department and Pia Bennett's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the Court's own motion to September 5, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. The Court exercises its discretion to consider the evidence introduced by Defendants with their reply, but affords Plaintiff Laura Torres an opportunity to respond to the new evidence. (See Jacobs v. Coldwell Banker Residential Brokerage Co. (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 438, 449 ["It is well established that the trial court's consideration of additional reply evidence is not an abuse of discretion so long as the party opposing the motion for summary judgment has notice and an opportunity to respond to the new material."] [internal quotations and citation omitted].) Plaintiff may respond to the reply evidence in advance of the new hearing date or at the hearing.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: HANFORD FREUND & COMPANY ET AL VS. HOMELAND INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF HANFORD FREUND & COMPANY, POWELL STREET PARTNERS, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiffs Hanford Freund & Company and Powell Partners, LLC's motion for summary adjudication is granted. Homeland Insurance Company is obligated to indemnify plaintiffs for the cost to repair the Powell Street property up to a blanket limit of $100,000,000 under the excess policy. The court concludes that defendant's unilateral attachment of the Loss Occurrence Limit of Liability Endorsement to the policy was not effective because defendant failed to provide plain, clear and conspicuous notice to plaintiffs of the potential reduction in coverage stemming from the endorsement. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY SHMIGELSKY VS. GETAROUND, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 15, DEFENDANT GETAROUND, INC., SAM ZAID, ADAM KOSMICKI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Getaround, Inc., Sam Zaid, and Adam Kosmicki's ("Getaround Defendants") demurrer to Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action for fraudulent concealment and constructive fraud, sustained with 30 days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for aiding and abetting insider trading, and overruled as to the fifth cause of action for securities fraud. The sixth cause of action for insider trading is brought only against the Purchaser Defendants, so the Getaround Defendants do not have standing to demur to that claim. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name, and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY SHMIGELSKY VS. GETAROUND, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 15, DEFENDANT MOHAMED ZAID, ILAN KROO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Ilan Kroo and Mohamed Zaid's demurrer to Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the third, fourth, and eighth causes of action for aiding and abetting fraud, civil conspiracy to defraud, and unjust enrichment and sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for insider trading. The third and fourth causes of action fail in light of the Court's ruling on the Getaround Defendants' demurrer dismissing the underlying fraud claims. Without a primary fraud claim, secondary causes of action such as aiding and abetting and conspiracy also fail. (See Richard B. LeVine, Inc. v. Higashi (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 566, 575-576 [claims for aiding and abetting and conspiracy fail if the underlying tort fails because both claims seek to vindicate the same primary right].) The eighth cause of action for unjust enrichment fails because such a claim does not lie where express binding agreements, such as the Stock Transfer Agreements, exist to define the rights of the parties. (Barnes Decl. Exs. A-E; see California Medical Assn., Inc. v. Aetna U.S. Healthcare of California, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 151, 172 ["[A]s a matter of law, a quasi-contract action for unjust enrichment does not lie where, as here, express binding agreements exist and define the parties' rights."].) The sixth cause of action for insider trading is pled with insufficient specificity. (FAC 70, 117 [allegations on information and belief that Purchaser Defendants committed insider trading based on purchase of stock and close familial and personal relationships with Getaround officers]; see In re VeriSign, Inc. Derivative Litigation (N.D. Cal. 2007) 531 F.Supp.2d 1173, 1221 [section 25402 claims should be pled with particularity "as insider trading under California law is a fraudulent practice"]; Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc. (2003) 30 Cal.4th 167, 184 ["In California, fraud must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice."] [citation omitted].) Generally alleging that Purchaser Defendants likely had access to information concealed from Plaintiff does not satisfy the particularity standard for this cause of action. Plaintiff has 30 days leave to amend to add specific factual allegations regarding the information allegedly shared by Getaround Defendants and obtained by each of the Purchaser Defendants. Furthermore, Plaintiff should allege if he can do so in good faith that Getaround has a class of equity security held of record by 500 or more persons as required to qualify for a remedy under Corporations Code section 25502.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name, and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES GUTIERREZ VS. OPORTUN FINANCIAL CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF JAMES GUTIERREZ's Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoenas served on his law firms (Nassiri & Jung LLP, Feldman Gale, and Cozen O'Connor) is granted, without prejudice to the issuance of new subpoenas that do not seek production of information protected from disclosure by the attorney-client privilege and/or the attorney work product doctrine. (See generally Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors v. Superior Court (2016) 2 Cal.5th 282, 293-300; County of Los Angeles Bd. of Supervisors v. Superior Court (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 1264, 1273-1276 [on remand].) By filing this action to enforce his written indemnity agreement with defendant, plaintiff did not put at issue privileged communications with his attorneys or effect an implied waiver of the attorney-client privilege. Because the subpoenas were oppressive and defendant opposed the motion without substantial justification, plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $9,870. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: *** TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY*** JONATHAN LAM VS. YI FENG GAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defts To Transfer Venue; Memo Of P&A; Supporting Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT YIFENG GAO's Motion By Defendant To Transfer Venue. Defendant Gao's motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Orange County and plaintiff is required to remit all required transfer costs. The court denies defendant's request for attorney's fees. (Code Civ. Proc. § 396b.) Plaintiff had a good faith basis for filing this action in San Francisco because that was defendant's last known address. (Finwall Decl., 3-4; Lam Decl., 10, 13.) In addition, plaintiff was willing to stipulate to the transfer but defendant ignored plaintiff's communications. (Finwall Decl., 8-10, 13-14, 16-18.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TODD J. FERRARIS VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT JEFFREY HUE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Jeffrey Hue's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for disability harassment of plaintiff Todd J. Ferraris' complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint fails to plead severe or pervasive harassing conduct sufficient to support a claim for disability discrimination. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
